economic_notes: Band operates Cannabis Boutique, Conne River Building Supplies, Conne River Garage, Conne River Gas Bar, Miawpukek Wholesalers, Micmac Arts and Crafts, Exploits Motel. Consolidated subsidiaries include Taqamkuk Development Corporation, Netukulimk Fisheries Ltd., Harbour Authority, and Exploits Motel. Band holds 7% ownership in First Nations Fisheries Interest Group LP at cost of $18,796,992. Economic activities revenue of $23,191,257. Outfitters operation noted.
notes: Fiscal year end is March 31, 2023 (not 2024). Province is Newfoundland and Labrador (NL). Four consolidated subsidiaries: Taqamkuk Development Corporation, Netukulimk Fisheries Ltd., Harbour Authority, Exploits Motel. ISC funding per Note 16 is $29,905,118; Health Canada funding is $8,453,913; CMHC is $190,563; total government transfers estimated as federal operating + federal capital + provincial = $37,020,207 per statement of operations, but Note 16 shows ISC $29,905,118 + Health Canada $8,453,913 + CMHC $190,563 = $38,549,594 plus provincial $1,685,306 = $40,234,900 approximately. OSR calculated as total revenue minus total government transfers. Demand loans of $4,677,651 also exist as short-term debt. Fishing licenses of $12,037,825 recorded as intangible non-financial assets (not amortized). Annual debt service reflects long-term debt repayment only; demand loan net repayment was $379,819. Remuneration schedule referenced but detail not included in extracted text. Revenue lines shown both as consolidated statement totals and Note 16 breakdown — duplication noted as Note 16 detail sums differ from statement totals due to classification differences. Drinking water class action settlement reserve of $428,400 appropriated during year.
